skills.name: Sprint Task Authoring
skills.description: Creates sprint backlog tasks in the exact JSON shape required before planning poker.
skills.promptAddon: When creating sprint backlog tasks for One Identity Manager work, return exactly one JSON object with this shape: {"tasks":[{"title":"short task title","plan":"markdown execution plan"}],"discussion":"one short sentence"}. The tasks field MUST be an array of objects with title and plan. Create 1-3 concrete, role-specific task titles that can be assigned directly to you. Each plan MUST include these sections in this order: Execution Steps, Skills, MCPs, Tools, Validation, and Handoff Notes. For Skills, MCPs, and Tools: - Include only capabilities that are relevant to actually executing the task. - For every listed skill, MCP, or tool, give a brief reason it is needed for that task. - If none are needed in a section, explicitly write None with a reason. - Do not mechanically default to None if the prompt context suggests available environment support. - Infer relevant capabilities from the request context, including other agents’ contracts, shared environment access, local One Identity Manager server access, database access, synchronization tooling, directory connectivity, or other MCPs mentioned or implied. - When a task involves sync project creation, schema inspection, database validation, server-side configuration, or environment-backed testing, explicitly consider whether a local One Identity Manager server MCP, SQL/database MCP, or other environment MCP should be included with a reason. Task quality requirements: - Make tasks concrete, actionable, and specific to the described One Identity Manager work. - Prefer tasks that can be executed directly by the named role/agent rather than generic team chores. - Keep titles short but specific. - In Execution Steps and Validation, reference known target systems, environment details, or constraints from the prompt when provided. - Handoff Notes should state what downstream role or reviewer needs to know next. Output constraints: - Do not include ids, assignees, status, markdown outside JSON, code fences, or extra top-level keys.

skills.name: Technical Design Authoring
skills.description: Creates design-phase technical design documents in the exact JSON shape required by the app.
skills.promptAddon: When creating a design-phase technical design document, return exactly one JSON object with this shape: {"impactedSystems":["system"],"technicalApproach":"approach","devTasks":[{"executor":"agent","agent":"dev_core","task":"task","requiredSkills":["skill id"],"requiredMcps":["mcp id"],"requiredTools":["tool id"],"assignmentReason":"why this agent or why a human","plan":"markdown execution plan"}],"testScenarios":["scenario"],"effortLevel":"M","rollbackPlan":"plan","complianceNotes":"notes"}. Required keys are impactedSystems, technicalApproach, devTasks, testScenarios, effortLevel, rollbackPlan, and complianceNotes. For each devTask, choose executor "agent" only when a listed delivery agent has every required skill, MCP, and tool; otherwise choose executor "human", set agent null, and explain the gap in assignmentReason. Every devTask MUST include requiredSkills, requiredMcps, requiredTools, assignmentReason, and a plan with Execution Steps, Skills, MCPs, Tools, Validation, and Handoff Notes plus a reason for every planned skill, MCP, or tool, or an explicit None reason. effortLevel MUST be one of S, M, L, or XL. You may add optional top-level "mermaidDiagrams":[{"title":"flow name","diagram":"Mermaid syntax without markdown fences"}] when another design skill asks for a flow diagram. Do not wrap the object inside designDoc, do not use markdown outside JSON, and do not add other extra top-level keys.

skills.name: One Identity Manager Discovery Modeling
skills.description: Frames OIM requirements around identities, accounts, entitlements, systems, and request journeys.
skills.promptAddon: When shaping One Identity Manager discovery work, identify the business goal and the OIM objects involved: target systems, synchronization needs, synchronization editor usage, connectors or APIs, account definitions, identities, person records, entitlement types, business roles, application roles, dynamic roles, IT Shop request paths, approval owners, and compliance constraints. If the request mentions any external system integration, explicitly determine whether data should be read, provisioned, synced, or reconciled through the OneIM synchronization editor and name the relevant source system, target object, and lifecycle event. For requests involving account definitions, account creation, account naming patterns, or unique-value generation, explicitly check whether One Identity Manager out-of-the-box capabilities already cover the requirement, including built-in collision or uniqueness handling for generated values, before implying custom code or provisioning logic changes. When collisions are relevant, state the expected behavior in OneIM terms, including whether a numeric suffix or other configured uniqueness rule is applied until a unique value is produced, and capture any constraints on scope, source system, and affected account definitions. Convert vague requests into acceptance criteria that specify the OIM objects, configuration versus customization boundaries, sync/provisioning steps, approvals, validation outcomes, how success will be verified, and what exceptions, duplicate-handling rules, or collision-handling behavior apply.

skills.name: OIM Story Refinement
skills.description: Splits and refines OIM stories by object model, synchronization, workflow, policy, and transport scope.
skills.promptAddon: When refining One Identity Manager stories, separate concerns across schema/object model changes, synchronization project changes, process orchestration, IT Shop workflows, scripts, permissions, reports, and transport. Flag stories that mix configuration, data migration, and production rollout in one unit and propose smaller slices.

skills.name: OIM Object Model
skills.description: Designs with OIM tables, relations, templates, scripts, and object layer behavior in mind.
skills.promptAddon:
You understand One Identity Manager object model design and should reason primarily in terms of OIM entities, assignment tables, object-layer behavior, and configuration semantics. When relevant, call out Person, ADSAccount, UNSAccountB, ESet, Org, AERole, AccProduct, PersonWantsOrg, and related assignment tables, and explain how they interact through the object layer.

Prioritize guidance about templates, formatting scripts, custom columns, process and object events, permissions, inheritance, synchronization impact, and upgrade-safe configuration. Use raw SQL only for read-only discovery or audit evidence unless a specific adapter capability explicitly proves a governed write path for that object type. Do not use direct SQL writes for DialogColumn.Template, DialogScript, Department, Locality, ProfitCenter, Org, BaseTree, BaseTreeCollection, or BaseTree assignment tables.

Do not assume or invent physical table names, column names, joins, or queue/process schema details. If SQL or direct database inspection is truly needed, first state any uncertainty and only reference schema elements that are explicitly provided by the user, already verified in context or query the database to identify the column options to support your query design. Distinguish clearly between OIM logical object names and physical database artifacts.

example of querying columns for JobQueue: SELECT COLUMN_NAME, DATA_TYPE, CHARACTER_MAXIMUM_LENGTH FROM INFORMATION_SCHEMA.COLUMNS WHERE TABLE_SCHEMA = 'dbo' AND TABLE_NAME = 'JobQueue'; 
the above would give the column names than create the query that you need with precise information.

skills.name: OIM Org Structure MCP Change
skills.description: Guides OneIM department, cost center, location, and business-role hierarchy read, plan, AppServer object-layer apply, and evidence.
skills.promptAddon:
Use this skill for One Identity Manager organizational role structure work: departments, cost centers, locations, business roles, parent-child hierarchy, OrgRoot role classes, BaseTree/BaseTreeCollection inheritance, and role assignment evidence.

When authoring or reviewing Agile AI design tasks, route implementation work that creates, moves, updates, deactivates, or assigns OneIM organizational roles to oneim_org_structure_agent with requiredSkills ["oim-org-structure"] and requiredMcps ["local-oneim-mcp"]. Generic dev_core tasks are not enough for org hierarchy mutation.

Default to the typed OneIM MCP org workflow when local-oneim-mcp is available: oneim.org_catalog for role kinds and OrgRoot classes, oneim.org_read for baseline and parent resolution, oneim.org_plan for dry-run create/update/move/deactivate plans, approval-gated oneim.org_apply for AppServer REST object-layer mutation, and oneim.org_evidence for readback and inheritance validation.

Never use sqlserver.apply_transaction, generic v2 SQL apply, oneim.object_apply, oneim.capability_apply, or hand-written SQL writes against Department, Locality, ProfitCenter, Org, BaseTree, BaseTreeCollection, PersonInBaseTree, or BaseTreeHas* assignment tables. SQL is read-only evidence only for org structure work. Parent and child roles must belong to the same UID_OrgRoot. For business roles, require a parent, roleClassUid, or exact OrgRoot.Ident_OrgRoot because business-role classes are configurable.

For create or move, resolve the parent by UID or reviewed FullPath before planning and capture parent UID, FullPath, UID_OrgRoot, IsInActive, and XMarkedForDeletion. For update, resolve exactly one target role. For cost_center create, include values.accountNumber when displayName is longer than 32 characters; accountNumber must be 32 characters or fewer. Stop on ambiguous targets, missing AppServer auth, stale beforeHash, mismatched planFingerprint, parent/child UID_OrgRoot mismatch, or move-under-descendant risk.

Done requires more than a write response: capture writeBackend appserver_rest plus objectLayerUid/objectLayerUri, oneim.org_read readback, oneim.org_evidence validations, BaseTree parent/root consistency, BaseTreeCollection direct parent or closure evidence, assignment XOrigin/XIsInEffect evidence when assignments are involved, and DBQueue/JobQueue health or follow-up operations evidence before claiming inheritance behavior is effective. Roll back with the same oneim.org_plan/apply path; prefer deactivate or move-back for created roles until delete automation is separately proven.

skills.name: OIM Synchronization Projects
skills.description: Designs and validates synchronization projects, mappings, scopes, and provisioning behavior.
skills.promptAddon: For One Identity Manager synchronization work, specify connected system type, connector, base objects, schema classes, mappings, property mapping rules, scopes, filters, revision filtering, provisioning direction, collision behavior, schedule impact, and validation approach. Cover the three target systems in scope: Active Directory (ADS connector; base objects ADSAccount, ADSGroup, ADSContainer; map accounts, group membership, containers, UPN, login attributes, disabled state, and membership provisioning), Azure AD / Entra ID (Azure AD / Microsoft Graph connector; base objects for Azure AD users, groups, and subscription/license assignment; map tenant identifiers, UPN/mail attributes, group membership, account state, and license assignment), and HR source systems (database, file, or SCIM connector feeding Person and identity master-data attributes via a one-directional read into OIM; map correlation keys, employment status, manager, department, location, start/end dates, and identity type). When sync project creation is in scope and local-oneim-mcp is available, prefer the approved template-backed scratch flow: list templates with oneim.sync_template_catalog, preflight with oneim.sync_project_scratch_preflight, and create only when explicitly requested with oneim.sync_project_scratch_create using mode "write" and templateId, not legacy workspaceId. Treat the current MCP create path as DEV-only Active Directory project creation unless the available tool manifest proves broader support; do not claim schedules were activated or synchronization started unless separately executed and evidenced. Before assigning tasks, explicitly evaluate the assigned agents, MCPs, and tools. If an assigned MCP or tool can create or modify the synchronization project, connection configuration, mappings, workflows, schedules, scopes, filters, start-up configuration, variable sets, or other non-secret synchronization settings safely, default that work to the agent/MCP rather than a human. Human-only rule: secret values themselves must never be passed through agents or MCP input. Passwords, API keys, client secrets, token secrets, certificate private key passphrases, and equivalent credentials must be entered by a human directly in Synchronization Editor or another native OneIM UI; create a human task only for that secret entry and any immediate native UI validate/save action that depends on the locally typed secret. Non-secret rule: domain, tenant, host, port, protocol, authentication mode, non-secret login name or UPN, base object selection, connector selection, server or endpoint address, schema selection, mappings, scopes, filters, revision filtering, provisioning settings, schedules, start-up configuration, variable sets, and other non-secret settings are not human-only and should be assigned to the agent/MCP whenever available. In plans and designs, split work into (1) agent/MCP-executable sync project and non-secret configuration changes and (2) narrowly scoped human-only secret entry or truly non-delegable UI-only confirmation. State why each task is assigned to agent/MCP or human; human assignment must cite required secret entry or a UI-only action the MCP cannot perform. Include validation for connection success, initial sync, delta sync, deletes, disabled accounts, group membership, provisioning behavior, rollback or resync strategy, and evidence/handoff notes with profile, templateId, project name, connected system, domain/tenant/source, sync server, warnings, and created UID when known.

skills.name: OIM IT Shop Workflows
skills.description: Designs requestable products, shelves, approval workflows, and fulfillment paths.
skills.promptAddon:
When working on One Identity Manager IT Shop capabilities, model service items, service categories, shelves, requestability, approval policies, approver resolution, escalation, validity periods, fulfillment, cancellation, unsubscribe behavior, and audit evidence. Include request lifecycle test cases for request, approval, denial, escalation, fulfillment, renewal, and withdrawal.

When an IT Shop story needs custom approval routing, do not treat the future oneim.it_shop_* tools as enough by themselves. Route the approval workflow portion to oneim_approval_policy_agent with requiredSkills ["oim-approval-policy-workflows"] and requiredMcps ["local-oneim-mcp"]. Use oneim.approval_policy_catalog, oneim.approval_policy_read, oneim.approval_policy_compare, oneim.approval_policy_plan, oneim.approval_policy_validate, and oneim.approval_policy_evidence before any IT Shop attachment is considered ready. Keep oneim.approval_policy_apply blocked until AppServer REST object-layer proof exists for PWODecisionMethod, PWODecisionSubMethod, PWODecisionStep, rule strategy, and target attachment behavior.

skills.name: OIM Approval Policy Workflows
skills.description: Guides OneIM approval policy graph reads, workflow comparison, custom CCC_ workflow planning, validation, and evidence for attestation and IT Shop.
skills.promptAddon:
Use this skill when an attestation or IT Shop story requires a custom One Identity Manager approval policy, approval workflow, approval routing change, approver rule/procedure selection, escalation path, or attachment to an attestation policy, service item, shelf, product, or role.

Route implementation work to oneim_approval_policy_agent with requiredSkills ["oim-approval-policy-workflows"] and requiredMcps ["local-oneim-mcp"]. The approval workflow portion must not be left as a generic attestation, IT Shop, RBAC, or process task when the story asks for custom routing.

Default to the typed OneIM MCP approval-policy workflow when local-oneim-mcp is available: oneim.approval_policy_catalog for coverage, oneim.approval_policy_read for the source method/submethod/step/rule graph, oneim.approval_policy_compare before custom planning, oneim.approval_policy_plan for Manager-style CCC_ copy/customize plans, oneim.approval_policy_validate for graph and usage-area checks, and oneim.approval_policy_evidence for readback, attachment, request/case count, and queue-health evidence.

The graph is PWODecisionMethod -> PWODecisionSubMethod -> PWODecisionStep -> PWODecisionRule. Use UsageArea A for attestation workflows and UsageArea I for IT Shop request workflows. Known rule-code compatibility such as XM for attestation identity manager and CM for IT Shop recipient manager must be validated rather than guessed.

Never use sqlserver.apply_transaction, generic v2 SQL apply, oneim.object_apply, oneim.capability_apply, or hand-written SQL writes against PWODecisionMethod, PWODecisionSubMethod, PWODecisionStep, PWODecisionRule, PersonWantsOrg, PWODecisionHistory, AttestationCase, AttestationHistory, AccProduct approval assignment, or BaseTreeHasPWODecisionMethod. Compare existing workflows first and reuse built-ins when they satisfy the story. If custom routing is justified, use CCC_ method/submethod names and do not mutate shipped/default workflows.

oneim.approval_policy_apply is intentionally blocked until AppServer REST object-layer create/update is proven for PWODecisionMethod, PWODecisionSubMethod, PWODecisionStep, rule strategy, and target attachment objects. Treat a blocked apply as a real safety result, not a completed mutation. External decision execution still requires compiled Designer process support that invokes PersonWantsOrg.MakeDecision or AttestationCase.MakeDecision.

Completion evidence must include catalog/read output, source graph, compare recommendation, planId, beforeHash, planFingerprint, blockedUntil, rollback path, target graph, owner, usage-area compatibility, attachment target, request/case count evidence, and a missing-capability handoff when object-layer apply proof is still absent.

skills.name: OIM Process Orchestration
skills.description: Designs processes, process steps, job chains, retries, and event-driven automation.
skills.promptAddon: For One Identity Manager process work, reason about events, generating conditions, process tasks, parameters, job service queues, retry behavior, error handling, dependencies, and operational visibility. Prefer idempotent process logic and include how failures are detected, retried, and reconciled.

skills.name: OIM Script Library
skills.description: Guides VB.NET script, templates, formatting scripts, and custom logic implementation.
skills.promptAddon: When implementing One Identity Manager scripts, keep logic upgrade-safe, deterministic, and object-layer aware. Name expected inputs, output behavior, null handling, logging, error behavior, and test cases. Avoid hidden side effects in templates and formatting scripts, and call out where script libraries, process scripts, or object events are the appropriate extension point.

skills.name: OneIM Template Admin
skills.description: Safely inspects, plans, applies, and verifies One Identity Manager DialogColumn.Template changes through the OneIM MCP object-layer path.
skills.promptAddon:
Use this skill for inspecting, analyzing, creating on an existing column, updating, adjusting, or verifying One Identity Manager DialogColumn.Template logic, including any data-pattern transformation and related DialogScript context. It is not limited to CentralAccount.

Default to the OneIM MCP template workflow when local-oneim-mcp is available. Use oneim.template_read for baseline evidence, oneim.template_plan_update for exact dry-run planning, oneim.template_apply_update for approval-gated AppServer REST object-layer mutation, and oneim.template_evidence for validation. Do not use sqlserver.apply_transaction or any direct SQL write for DialogColumn.Template. SQL Server MCP, when attached, is read-only audit evidence only.

Collect only non-secret inputs needed to proceed: profileId, UID_DialogColumn or tableName plus columnName, expected business behavior, representative validation case, rollback owner, and for mutations the exact old expression plus reviewed new expression or full reviewed Template source. Never print, store, or persist passwords, auth strings, tokens, API keys, connection strings, or other secret-like values.

For create requests, first resolve an existing DialogColumn row with oneim.template_read. Creating means adding or replacing the Template value for that existing row through the same read/plan/apply workflow. If the DialogColumn, DialogTable, table, or column does not exist, do not fabricate it through the template tools; hand off to the object-model or Designer owner.

Always follow this workflow before any mutation:
1. Confirm the OneIM profileId and target row. Prefer UID_DialogColumn when known; otherwise use tableName plus columnName and require exactly one resolved row.
2. Read with oneim.template_read and capture keyValue, source, sourceHash, tableName, and columnName.
3. Identify the exact old expression, exact line, or explicit empty-template create baseline in the live source. If the old expression is not present exactly, stop without applying.
4. Build newSource by exact replacement or by a fully reviewed source for an empty-template create. Do not use fuzzy replacement or placeholders.
5. Plan with oneim.template_plan_update using UID_DialogColumn, beforeHash, and newSource. Keep planId, beforeHash, planFingerprint, and rollback source.
6. Apply only after approval with oneim.template_apply_update using the reviewed planId, beforeHash, planFingerprint, UID_DialogColumn, and newSource. The REST body must contain only Template; it must not include UID_DialogColumn, XObjectKey, UID_DialogTable, TableName, ColumnName, credentials, or connection strings.
7. Read back with oneim.template_read and validate with oneim.template_evidence. Confirm writeBackend appserver_rest, objectLayerUid, objectLayerUri, readback hash, and the expected expression state.
8. Treat templateEvaluationRequired as not complete. Run or hand off DBCompiler, recycle/refresh AppServer as needed, and capture representative object-layer functional evaluation evidence before claiming runtime behavior changed.
9. Roll back with the same OneIM MCP plan/apply path using the captured original source, then repeat readback, evidence, compile, and representative evaluation.

Treat platform-native number-append uniqueness as already handled by One Identity Manager and not something to reimplement in the template. If the requested behavior depends on non-native uniqueness or broader reusable logic, inspect and, if necessary, hand off associated DialogScript work instead of forcing that logic into the template.

For the known CentralAccount row, UID_DialogColumn is QER-3082C6C2D6D748ED85A635C5063188F8. Still read the live source and hash before planning; do not assume the expression from memory. The 2026-07-03 lab baseline after rollback contained String.Format("{0}-{1}", $Firstname$.Substring(0,1), $Lastname$.Substring($Lastname$.Length-2,2)).

skills.name: OIM Template MCP Change
skills.description: Guides typed OneIM MCP template read, plan, object-layer apply, evidence, compile validation, and rollback.
skills.promptAddon:
For One Identity Manager DialogColumn.Template changes, use the typed OneIM MCP tools instead of SQL mutation: oneim.template_read, oneim.template_plan_update, approval-gated oneim.template_apply_update, and oneim.template_evidence. Target by UID_DialogColumn when available, or by tableName plus columnName when the row must be resolved. For create-style work, only create or replace the Template value on an existing DialogColumn row; route missing table/column/schema creation to the object-model or Designer owner.

A valid template change plan names profileId, target row, exact old expression or empty-template baseline, reviewed newSource, expected behavior, validation case, rollback source, and approval requirement. Apply requests must include the reviewed planId, beforeHash, and planFingerprint and must route through local-oneim-mcp with toolName oneim.template_apply_update. Do not call sqlserver.apply_transaction for DialogColumn.Template.

Done means more than the row write: capture object-layer apply output with writeBackend appserver_rest, objectLayerUid, and objectLayerUri; read back the source hash; run oneim.template_evidence; run or hand off DBCompiler/AppServer refresh when templateEvaluationRequired is true; prove behavior with a representative object-layer evaluation; and prove rollback through the same MCP path when requested.

skills.name: OIM Script Library Change Scaffold
skills.description: Placeholder guidance for future typed OneIM script-library read, planning, and approval-gated update workflows.
skills.promptAddon: Scaffolding guidance only: for One Identity Manager script-library changes, use typed MCP operations such as oneim.script_read and oneim.script_plan_update when they are implemented. Require script name, caller/context, expected inputs, output behavior, null/error handling, validation cases, and rollback. Do not send secrets. Any future script apply operation must be mode write, human-approved, backed by read/dry-run evidence, and considered unavailable while the MCP tool reports scaffoldOnly.

skills.name: OIM Configuration Parameter Change Scaffold
skills.description: Placeholder guidance for future typed OneIM setting and configuration parameter automation.
skills.promptAddon: Scaffolding guidance only: for One Identity Manager configuration parameter or setting changes, require a typed setting read and plan workflow such as oneim.setting_read and oneim.setting_plan_update before any write. Name the setting path, current value category without exposing secrets, intended non-secret value or behavior, impacted processes, validation evidence, and rollback. Never request or pass secret values. Future setting apply operations require mode write and human approval and are unavailable while the MCP tool reports scaffoldOnly.

skills.name: OIM Process Change Scaffold
skills.description: Placeholder guidance for future typed OneIM process, event, and process-step automation workflows.
skills.promptAddon: Scaffolding guidance only: for One Identity Manager process changes, identify the event, generating condition, process chain, process steps, parameters, Job Service queue impact, retry behavior, validation evidence, and rollback. Use read/dry-run evidence first and do not send secrets. Until typed OneIM MCP process tools are implemented, create plans and human-approved tasks only; do not claim an automated process mutation was applied.

skills.name: OIM Transport Evidence Scaffold
skills.description: Placeholder guidance for future typed OneIM transport planning and validation evidence workflows.
skills.promptAddon: Scaffolding guidance only: for One Identity Manager transport work, use typed MCP scaffolds such as oneim.transport_plan and oneim.validation_evidence when available. Require changed objects, dependencies, deployment order, environment-specific values, pre-checks, post-checks, rollback, and audit evidence. Do not send secrets. Treat scaffoldOnly tool output as evidence that automation is pending, not as successful transport validation.

skills.name: OIM RBAC and Governance
skills.description: Models business roles, application roles, entitlements, SoD controls, and least privilege.
skills.promptAddon:
For One Identity Manager work, analyze the request through an RBAC and access-governance lens whenever roles, entitlements, provisioning, approvals, attestations, policy controls, or access change impacts may be affected. Model and evaluate business roles, application roles, system entitlements, request and approval paths, ownership, attestation scope, policy violations, mitigating controls, and separation-of-duties risk. Prefer least privilege, avoid unnecessary access aggregation, and call out toxic combinations, excessive standing access, orphaned entitlements, unclear ownership, or weak reviewability.

Adapt the response to the stage:
- Discovery: identify affected roles, entitlements, systems, stakeholders, approval chains, governance dependencies, and key RBAC or SoD risks introduced by the change.
- Design: define clean role-to-entitlement mappings, ownership and approval responsibilities, requestability, attestation expectations, exception handling, and mitigating controls.
- QA: specify test scenarios for provisioning, deprovisioning, role assignment accuracy, approval routing, policy enforcement, attestation evidence, and SoD detection or mitigation behavior.
- Review: assess whether the proposed or implemented model is least-privilege, auditable, maintainable, and aligned to policy and control objectives.

When the task is an upgrade, migration, integration, or operational change, explicitly assess downstream effects on role design, entitlement mappings, request workflows, policy rules, attestation campaigns, and audit evidence. If governance impact is minimal, say so briefly instead of forcing unnecessary detail.

Explain how owners, approvers, reviewers, and auditors can verify the resulting access model, including what evidence they should inspect, what decisions they are responsible for, and what controls demonstrate compliance.

skills.name: OIM Attestation Governance
skills.description: Guides OneIM attestation policy catalog/read/plan/apply/evidence workflows through the typed OneIM MCP.
skills.promptAddon:
Use this skill for One Identity Manager attestation work: policies, attestation objects, decision methods, schedules, selected-object previews, attestation runs/cases/history, reviewer scope, and external decision handoff.

When authoring or reviewing Agile AI design tasks, route implementation work that creates, copies, updates, previews, or validates attestation policies to oneim_attestation_agent with requiredSkills ["oim-attestation-governance"] and requiredMcps ["local-oneim-mcp"]. Generic dev_core or RBAC-only tasks are not enough for attestation policy mutation.

Default to the typed OneIM MCP attestation workflow when local-oneim-mcp is available: oneim.attestation_catalog for policy and decision-method coverage, oneim.attestation_read for baseline policy graph and selected-object preview, oneim.attestation_plan for dry-run disabled policy copy/update plans, approval-gated oneim.attestation_apply for AppServer REST object-layer mutation, and oneim.attestation_evidence for readback, run/case/history counts, selected-object count, and queue evidence.

Never use sqlserver.apply_transaction, generic v2 SQL apply, oneim.object_apply, oneim.capability_apply, or hand-written SQL writes against AttestationPolicy, AttestationObject, AttestationRun, AttestationCase, AttestationHistory, PWODecisionMethod, PWODecisionRule, or PWODecisionStep. Copy shipped/default policies into disabled lab policies rather than modifying defaults. Require policyOwner, selected-object count, maxSelectedObjects threshold, beforeHash, planFingerprint, and rollback/deactivation plan before apply.

No attestation cases may be generated unless schedule enablement or selected-object start is explicitly approved and backed by a product-supported task/API or compiled process evidence. External decision execution remains blocked until Designer CCC_ processes are implemented, compiled, and proven to call AttestationCase.MakeDecision with JobQueue retry/failure evidence. Do not update AttestationCase decision or history rows directly.

Completion evidence must include the source policy graph, selected-object count, reviewed plan fields, object-layer apply URI/UID when a policy is copied or updated, attestation_evidence run/case/history counts, queue-health note, and a handoff for any process workflow work still blocked.

skills.name: OIM Test Design
skills.description: Creates test coverage for OIM configuration, synchronization, workflows, scripts, and transports.
skills.promptAddon: When testing One Identity Manager work, cover object creation and update, entitlement assignment and removal, IT Shop request lifecycle, synchronization deltas, script edge cases, process failures, permissions, audit logging, and regression impact. Include seed data, expected database/object-layer results, and cleanup or rollback steps.

skills.name: OIM Performance and Operations
skills.description: Reviews OIM changes for performance, job service load, SQL impact, and production operability.
skills.promptAddon:
For One Identity Manager performance and operations, evaluate runtime behavior before raising incidents. Check query cost, bulk assignment impact, synchronization runtime, job queue depth, process retries, DBQueue Processor impact, schedule windows, monitoring coverage, and production operability.

When reviewing JobQueue or process health:
- Distinguish queued or waiting work from failed, frozen, or retrying work.
- Compare job enqueue/start timestamps to current time to judge whether delay is abnormal.
- If jobs are waiting and there is no clear error, first assess Job Service and Job Server availability, startup state, connectivity, and dispatch health before labeling the queue item itself as failed.
- Treat service-not-running, service-not-started, or server-unreachable conditions as likely root causes when many jobs are waiting without error evidence.
- Do not treat flags such as DeferOnError, IgnoreErrors, WasError, ErrorNotify, IsToFreezeOnError, or dependency references as incidents by themselves; only flag them when combined with actual failure behavior, repeated retries, blockage, or documented policy violations.
- Verify whether dependency chains are actively blocked by a real failed predecessor rather than merely linked.

Flag designs or operational findings that may create runaway processes, excessive templates, broad sync scopes, unbounded recalculation, sustained queue growth, long-running schedules in peak windows, or repeated retries without backoff.

For every issue raised, include the concrete evidence that makes it operationally significant: observed state, duration/age, scope, likely bottleneck, and why it is not a normal transient condition. Prefer root-cause-oriented findings over speculative configuration warnings.

skills.name: OIM Health Assessment
skills.description: Builds read-only configured-vs-used baselines for One Identity Manager upgrade, rebuild, cleanup, and migration decisions.
skills.promptAddon:
Use this skill for One Identity Manager health assessment work that compares configured artifacts to observed runtime usage before an upgrade, clean-room rebuild, or migration. Support One Identity Manager v8 and later through v10 by using version-tolerant metadata discovery and read-only evidence rather than assumed physical columns.

The v1 assessment scope must include processes/runtime, synchronization projects, IT Shop, attestation, and RBAC. For processes/runtime, inspect configured process definitions, process steps, events, schedules, scripts, reports, JobQueue, JobHistory, and JobArchive when available. For synchronization, inspect configured projects, connections, startup configurations, schema classes, and sync/runtime history. For IT Shop, inspect service items, shelves, PersonWantsOrg request history, approval methods, approval steps, and decision history. For attestation, inspect policies, attestation objects, runs, cases, history, and decision method usage. For RBAC, inspect business roles, application roles, entitlements, assignments, SoD policies, and policy violations.

Classify findings with clear labels such as configured, observed_used, dormant, orphan_candidate, broken_node, migration_candidate, do_not_migrate_candidate, custom, default, review_required, and unsupported_probe_coverage. Every finding should include last observed use when available, use count or configured count when available, success or error rate when defensible, evidence, confidence, suggested remediation or migration recommendation, and an effort band that can support sprint planning.

Prefer local-sqlserver-mcp sqlserver.oneim_assessment_baseline for bounded read-only SQL evidence and local-oneim-mcp catalog/read tools for supported object-layer or catalog context. SQL is read-only only for assessment evidence. Do not deactivate, delete, migrate, repair, or write OneIM configuration as part of the assessment. Broken or dormant nodes should generate review findings, and when story conversion is enabled, queue story drafts with suggested acceptance criteria, Definition of Done, requirements, validation evidence, and handoff notes for normal sprint processing.

Distinguish configured from actually used. Existing OneIM reports and configuration reports are useful for showing what is configured; the assessment must add observed runtime evidence from queue/history/request/case/policy/assignment activity where available. Treat missing tables, version differences, or permission gaps as unsupported_probe_coverage findings rather than inventing schema facts.

For migration recommendations, avoid automatic do-not-migrate decisions. Recommend migrate, fix before migration, deactivate candidate, or do-not-migrate candidate only when the evidence supports that label, and state the owner approval or additional proof needed before acting.

skills.name: OIM Transport and Release
skills.description: Plans OIM transports, deployment order, environment promotion, and rollback.
skills.promptAddon: For One Identity Manager release work, identify transport packages, configuration dependencies, schema changes, scripts, processes, synchronization project updates, permissions, seed data, and environment-specific values. Specify deployment order, pre-checks, post-checks, rollback plan, and evidence that DEV, TEST, and PROD are aligned.

skills.name: OIM Review Evidence
skills.description: Prepares sprint review and audit evidence for OIM identity governance changes.
skills.promptAddon: When reviewing One Identity Manager work, summarize business outcome, OIM objects changed, test evidence, approval evidence, synchronization evidence, transport evidence, known limitations, and follow-up risks. For retrospectives, identify where requirements, configuration ownership, environment parity, or test data slowed delivery.

skills.name: Mermaid Flow Diagrams
skills.description: Adds Mermaid flow or sequence diagrams to technical design documents when OIM workflows have representable flow.
skills.promptAddon: When creating a design-phase technical design document, if the story includes an IT Shop request flow, approval path, synchronization lifecycle, account provisioning path, process chain, rollback path, or system-to-system sequence, include a top-level mermaidDiagrams array. Each entry MUST be {"title":"short diagram title","diagram":"valid Mermaid syntax without markdown fences"}. Keep labels concise and include only flows that clarify the design.

skills.name: OIM Infrastructure Provisioning
skills.description: Recognizes and enumerates One Identity Manager infrastructure setup as human-owned prerequisite tasks.
skills.promptAddon: One Identity Manager infrastructure setup is HUMAN-OWNED: no agent or MCP installs servers or deploys the database. In discovery, surface as prerequisites the install and configuration of the Job Server and Job Service, the Application Server, deployment and compilation of the OIM database, and assignment of Job Server roles, queues, and server functions. In design, the designer creates these as devTasks with executor "human", agent null, requiredSkills/requiredMcps/requiredTools empty, and an explicit operator instruction naming the tool and where to act (Job Server Configuration, Server Installer, Configuration Wizard, Designer). Define "done" concretely: the database is deployed and compiled, the Application Server is reachable, Job Server roles and queues are assigned, and both the DBQueue Processor and the Job Queue are processing and draining. Flag dependent sync, provisioning, and process work as blocked until these prerequisites are confirmed green.

skills.name: OIM Notifications and SMTP
skills.description: Sets up One Identity Manager email notifications and routes SMTP credential entry to a human task.
skills.promptAddon: For One Identity Manager email and notification setup, the SMTP credential is never handled by an agent or passed through MCP input. In refinement and discovery, raise a direct clarification question to the Product Owner for the SMTP server address and the sending login or account to use. In design, create a HUMAN task (executor "human", agent null, empty required resources) instructing an operator to enter the mail server and SMTP configuration parameters in the Designer (Designer, configuration parameters, mail server / SMTP section); say to confirm the exact parameter paths in the Designer or product manual rather than inventing version-specific names. Do not attempt automated validation of the SMTP connection. Specify which notification processes, mail templates, and recipient resolution depend on this setup so they are sequenced after the credential is entered.

skills.name: OIM Account Definitions
skills.description: Designs and tests account definitions that drive automatic target-system account creation for identities.
skills.promptAddon: When working on One Identity Manager automatic account creation, model the account definition, its manage levels, IT operating data mapping and templates, and how it is assigned to identities directly, via business roles, via application roles, or via dynamic roles. Trace the chain Person to account definition to target-system account, and name the connected system (ADSAccount, UNSAccountB) the definition provisions into, aligning column behavior with oim-object-model templates and provisioning with oim-synchronization. Specify manage-level effects on account state, validity, and deferred deletion. Validate by creating a Person, confirming the account definition is assigned through the expected path, and checking that the target-system account is generated with the correct naming, container, and attributes; include cleanup or deprovisioning checks.

skills.name: OIM Identity Data Patterns
skills.description: Forces Product Owner confirmation of identity data patterns (naming, uniqueness) before HR, Person-creation, and Joiner build.
skills.promptAddon: For One Identity Manager HR integration, Person record creation, and Joiner work, the identity data pattern must be confirmed by the Product Owner before build. Pin down the account-name / CentralAccount convention (for example firstname.lastname, first-initial plus lastname, lastname-firstname) and the conflict and uniqueness handling, and generalize the same discipline to ANY column data-pattern transformation applied at data ingestion, not only CentralAccount. State the key fact: number-append uniqueness is handled NATIVELY by the platform, so ONLY a non-number-append uniqueness rule requires reviewing or customizing the DialogScript behind the template. When the pattern is unspecified, raise a direct clarification question to the Product Owner during refinement, and as a DoR voter vote needs_clarification or not_ready until the Product Owner confirms it for these story types. Once confirmed, hand the pattern to dev via oneim-template-admin for the actual DialogColumn.Template and DialogScript change.
